message:
test(ffs-cli): add btrfs CLI E2E coverage

Extends the existing cli_e2e.rs suite with btrfs-backed end-to-end
checks so the CLI's btrfs paths are exercised alongside the ext4 ones
that the ext4 prerequisite helper already covers.

- btrfs_prerequisites_available(): mirror of cli_prerequisites_available
  for the btrfs side — just checks whether mkfs.btrfs is on PATH so the
  tests can SKIP cleanly on hosts without btrfs-progs installed rather
  than hard-failing.

- create_minimal_btrfs_image(dir, size_mb): new helper that mirrors the
  ext4 helper pattern — dd a sparse file, run `mkfs.btrfs -f -q` to
  lay down a clean btrfs filesystem image at the given size, and
  return the image path. Uses stdio null-redirect so test output stays
  clean.

- cli_inspect_btrfs_subvolumes: first btrfs E2E — builds a 128 MB
  image and runs `ffs inspect --subvolumes <image>`, asserting exit
  success. Skips with a clear SKIP message when mkfs.btrfs is absent.
  On failure, emits a structured scenario-result line before
  panicking so the harness logs show why.

- Also lands a second fsck-style scenario that runs `ffs fsck` against
  a clean btrfs image and asserts exit success, matching the ext4
  fsck-clean-image scenario already in the suite.

No production code changes.
